raw_consumer_statement:
XX/XX/year> : I filed a complaint with the CFPB ( Case # XXXX for Citi ) XXXX Citibank and XXXX maintained their positions. 7Citi violated the Fair Credit Billing Act by denying a valid dispute for undelivered/stolen merchandise and refusing to provide a refund. 


What happened : I am filing another complaint against Citibank for violating federal law under the Fair Credit Billing Act ( FCBA ) by improperly denying my billing dispute for {$1000.00} charged to my My Best Buy Visa Platinum card on XX/XX/year>, for a purchase made from XXXX XXXX. 

The item was allegedly delivered by the shipping carrier, but it was stolen before I ever took possession. I have video evidence showing the theft and filed a police report immediately. Despite this, Citibank refused to credit the charge, claiming that since the package was delivered, they fulfilled their obligation. This response is legally incorrect and demonstrates a clear disregard for federal consumer protections. 

The FCBA explicitly protects cardholders from being charged for merchandise not received. Courts and the CFPB have confirmed that non-receipt includes situations where the item was stolen before the cardholder had physical possession. I made a timely and proper dispute, but Citibank dismissed it entirely, and their response dated XX/XX/year> reflects negligence, indifference to the law, and predatory banking behavior. 

Additionally, the Office of the Comptroller of the Currency ( OCC ) reviewed my case and referred me directly to the CFPB, acknowledging this matter falls under your jurisdiction. Citis refusal to correct this charge forces consumers like me to pay for merchandise we never receivedthis is unfair, deceptive, and abusive under federal consumer financial protection laws.
